chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No.D- 718 of 2025 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Before: Mr. Justice Muhammad Saleem Jessar Mr. Justice Riazat Ali Sahar Petitioner: Hashim son of Allah Bux Chandio through Mr. Deewan Dhanraj, Advocate. The Respondents: The State and Province of Sindh through M/s Zulfiqar Ahmed Jatoi, Additional P.G and Zulfiqar Ali Naich, Assistant A.G. Date of Hearing: 09-07-2025 Date of Decision: 09-07-2025 O R D E R RIAZAT ALI SAHAR, J. By way of this petition, petitioner Hashim son of Allah Bux by caste Chandio, seeks his release on post arrest bail in crime No.147 of 2025 under section 9 (i), 3(b) Sindh CNS Act, 2024, registered at P.S, Moro, for allegedly possessing 550 grams of Charas. 2. Since facts of the prosecution case are already mentioned in the FIR as well as in the memo of petition; therefore, there is no need to reproduce the same. Reliance is placed on the case of Muhammad Shakeel v. The State & others (PLD 2014 SC 458). 3. Record reflects that interim challan was submitted by the police before the Court having jurisdiction. Since the statute does not provide a specific provision for filing a bail application, and there being no alternate remedy, the petitioner has maintained this petition. 4. Learned counsel for the petitioner argued that the alleged recovery of contraband substance, viz. Charas weighing 550 grams, is a meager quantity. He next submitted, although the punishment for offence under the Sindh CNS Act, 2024 is “imprisonment which may extend to nine years but shall not be less than five years”. Since, the lesser quantum of sentence has been provided which does not fall

He next submitted, although the punishment for offence under the Sindh CNS Act, 2024 is “imprisonment which may extend to nine years but shall not be less than five years”. Since, the lesser quantum of sentence has been provided which does not fall 2 within the ambit of prohibitory clause of Section 497 Cr.P.C, hence he contended that the case against the petitioner requires further inquiry and prayed for grant of bail. 5. Learned Law Officers, in view of above facts and circumstances, have recorded their no objection. 6. We have heard learned counsel for the petitioner as well as law officers and have gone through the material made available before us on record. 7. Allegedly, the petitioner has been nominated in the FIR, and 550 grams of contraband substance, viz. Charas, is shown to have been recovered from his possession. However, the punishment provided by law for the said offence is the imprisonment which may extend to nine years but shall not be less than five years. As per the settled principle, when a statute provides two sets of punishment, the lesser one is to be considered at the bail stage. Hence, the alleged offence does not fall within the ambit of prohibitory clause of Section 497 Cr.P.C, thus making bail the rule and jail the exception. 8. In the circumstances and in view of above legal as well as factual position of record the petitioner has succeeded to make out a good prima facie case for grant of bail. The case of petitioner is purely covered by Section 497 Cr.P.C. Resultantly, instant Petition was allowed by our short order dated 09.07.2025, whereby the petitioner Hashim Chandio was ordered to be released on bail subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Fifty Thousand) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Sessions Judge, Naushahro Feroze/trial Court, as the case may be. These are the reasons in support of our above short order. 9.

Resultantly, instant Petition was allowed by our short order dated 09.07.2025, whereby the petitioner Hashim Chandio was ordered to be released on bail subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Fifty Thousand) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Sessions Judge, Naushahro Feroze/trial Court, as the case may be. These are the reasons in support of our above short order. 9. Needless to mention here that the observations recorded hereinabove are tentative in nature and shall not, in any manner, prejudice or influence the trial Court during the adjudication of the case. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmad/P.S
